{"key_event":"Watergate and post-Vietnam oversight reforms","reasoning":"Civil society gained leverage through antiwar, rights, environmental, taxpayer, evangelical, media, and public-interest mobilization, though weakening unions and parties kept the increase modest. The federal state remained extraordinarily capable and expanded regulation, but Vietnam, Watergate, fiscal stress, FOIA/FISA/Congressional oversight, tax revolt, and deregulation reduced executive legitimacy and unilateral capacity from the late-1960s peak.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":8.8,"state_change":-0.4,"state_power":9.3}
